$schema_note: Flat map of repo-relative file path -> {description, depends_on, dependents, system}. depends_on/dependents are computed from real imports for .py and .ts/.tsx files (via tree-sitter) -- other files have empty lists. A brand-new file gets a draft stub (description prefixed '[SIN VERIFICAR]', system possibly 'UNASSIGNED') until scripts/draft_file_map_entries.py's LLM pass confirms or corrects it (then the description is prefixed '[DRAFT sin revisar]' instead, and the temporary `_draft_candidate` key is removed). rayflow/editor/static/dist/ (compiled bundle) is intentionally excluded. Generated by scripts/generate_file_map.py (pre-commit hook `file-map-generate`); not wired into CLAUDE.md.
root: rayflow — visual workflow orchestration on Ray

.claude/agents/rayflow-auditor.md.description: Repo-dev subagent (not shipped to end users, unlike rayflow/claude_tools/agents/rayflow-debugger.md): verifies RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims against the real repo state and detects contradictions. Scoped to a diff via .claude/hooks/_sot_scope.py (claim ids by default, `--docs` for the affected documentation files), or run unscoped over the full SOT for a manual audit. When filling an issue's `docs`, starts from the claim's own `docs` field and adds anything its own search finds missing (reported, not written back to the SOT). No longer has Edit: read-only, never touches RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json, CLAUDE.md, or rayflow_issues.json itself — reports every confirmed finding to .claude/agents/rayflow-issue-writer.md, the only agent with write permission on rayflow_issues.json, instead of writing there directly (unless run as part of a parallel fan-out, in which case it just returns the requested proposed_issues data structure). No longer has Bash either — delegates any shell command it needs (e.g. running _sot_scope.py itself for an unscoped/manual run) to rayflow-bash-runner via the Agent tool.

.claude/agents/rayflow-github-runner.md.description: Repo-dev subagent: the only agent in this repo with the `mcp__github__*` tools (PRs, issues, reviews, CI, branches) in its frontmatter — same pattern as rayflow_bash_runner.md but for GitHub operations instead of shell. Concentrates the blast radius of remote GitHub operations in a single auditable place; every other agent delegates GitHub work to it instead of holding those tools itself.

.claude/agents/rayflow-hooks-infra-speci.description: GENERATED FILE (scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py, pre-commit hook `agents-generate` — never edit by hand, overwritten on the next commit if stale). See rayflow_agents_system.md.

.claude/agents/rayflow-issue-moderator.m.description: Repo-dev subagent: given an issue_id from rayflow_issues.json, moderates a discussion among the relevant rayflow-<system>-specialist agents (one if a single system is involved, several with up to 2-3 rounds of cross-specialist objections/adjustments if not) to design the best fix, and returns a Markdown solution plan in its final text response — never persisted anywhere. Read-only like rayflow-router/rayflow-auditor (no Edit): never touches source files, never edits rayflow_issues.json (doesn't delete the issue or add a suggested_fix, a field the schema deliberately omits), and never asks a specialist to implement anything — pure design discussion, not execution. If specialists don't converge within the round cap, the moderator itself decides and states the disagreement and its resolution explicitly in the plan.

.claude/agents/rayflow-issue-writer.md.description: Repo-dev subagent: the only agent in this repo with write permission on rayflow_issues.json. Any other agent (rayflow-auditor, the rayflow-<system>-specialists, rayflow-router, or anyone else) that spots a possible discrepancy between a RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claim and the real code reports the finding here instead of editing the file directly — whether the finding came from a formal audit or was incidental to other work. Independently re-verifies each candidate against RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json and the real repo state before writing anything; does not blindly trust the report it receives. The consolidation phase of .claude/workflows/audit-sot-parallel.js now runs as an instance of this agent rather than of rayflow-auditor, since it performs the single atomic write to rayflow_issues.json.

.claude/agents/rayflow-main.md.description: Repo-dev subagent definition loaded as the default main-session identity via the `agent` field in .claude/settings.json — replaces Claude Code's default system prompt for every new session in this repo (CLAUDE.md/project memory still loads normally on top of it). Toolset: Agent, SendMessage, Task* (6 tools), AskUserQuestion, Workflow, ToolSearch (the latter only to load deferred schemas), plus its own Edit/Read and all 26 mcp__Rayflow__* tools (Rayflow's remote account-level OAuth Connector) — the latter work here specifically because account Connector tools only propagate to whichever agent runs as the session's main thread, never to subagents spawned via Agent, confirmed empirically via rayflow-mcp-runner.md (identical declared tool set, could never see them; retired as a result). Still no Grep/Glob/Bash/Skill/WebFetch/Artifact/mcp__github__*/mcp__Claude_Code_Remote__* — those and any system-specific work stay delegated to the matching rayflow-<system>-specialist, rayflow-router, rayflow-github-runner, or rayflow-bash-runner. Also documents a catalog of pre-made `.claude/workflows/*.js` scripts (referenced by absolute scriptPath, not by name — name-based Workflow-tool resolution proved unreliable for newly added files) for tasks with a known orchestration shape; having a workflow cataloged here does not itself authorize firing it — the Workflow tool's own explicit user opt-in gate still applies every turn, so this agent must surface the match and wait for the user to confirm/name it before calling Workflow.

.claude/agents/rayflow-mcp-specialist.md.description: GENERATED FILE (scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py, pre-commit hook `agents-generate` — never edit by hand, overwritten on the next commit if stale). See rayflow_agents_system.md.

.claude/agents/rayflow-pylib-inspector.m.description: Repo-dev subagent (manual definition, not generated by scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py): answers questions about the real runtime behavior of a Python library — especially pinned to a specific VERSION — by inspecting a real installation's source/stubs/introspection (and, as a secondary/experimental signal only, `ty`) instead of relying on training-memory recall or web prose. Inspects the version already installed in whatever environment is currently active by default (faster, no setup cost); only isolates in a temp /tmp venv when an explicitly different version is requested, the library isn't installed at all, or touching the current environment risks breaking the repo's own env. Complementary to rayflow-web-researcher.md, not a replacement: this one is for verifiable facts about installed code, not documentation/tutorials/community opinion. Sole tool Bash — unlike rayflow_bash_runner.md (executes exactly what it's told, doesn't decide what to run), this agent does decide its own investigation sequence.

.claude/agents/rayflow-router.md.description: Repo-dev subagent: read-only (Read, Grep, Glob, Agent, SendMessage — no Edit) agent that locates which system/specialist a vague question or task belongs to, for when rayflow-main (which no longer carries Read/Grep/Glob of its own) can't infer it directly from the systems index in CLAUDE.md/rayflow_file_map.json. Never modifies anything — only searches and reports which rayflow-<system>-specialist (or other agent) should handle the request.

.claude/agents/rayflow-schema-specialist.description: GENERATED FILE (scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py, pre-commit hook `agents-generate` — never edit by hand, overwritten on the next commit if stale). See rayflow_agents_system.md.

.claude/agents/rayflow-web-researcher.md.description: Repo-dev subagent (manual definition, not generated by scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py): general-purpose online research, not tied to any rayflow system — searches and reads real web pages (tools, products, papers, external APIs, anything) and synthesizes findings with cited sources. Deliberately restricted to WebSearch + WebFetch only: no Read/Grep/Glob/Edit (no repo filesystem access) and no Agent/SendMessage (can't delegate to other agents).

.claude/agents/rayflow_bash_runner.md.description: Repo-dev subagent: the only agent in this repo with Bash in its frontmatter. Every other rayflow-* subagent (the 21 rayflow-<system>-specialist agents plus rayflow-auditor) has Agent + SendMessage instead of Bash, and delegates shell command execution to this agent — spawn via Agent for the first command, then SendMessage to the same agent id/name to keep running more commands in the same conversation without losing context. Deliberately narrow tool surface (Bash only): executes exactly what it's asked and reports command + exit code + output, doesn't decide what to run.

.claude/hooks/_file_map.py.description: Shared helper module (not a hook itself) used by every hook script here: loads rayflow_file_map.json, resolves an absolute file_path to a repo-relative path, extracts live function/class signatures from a .py file via ast (never cached, so it can't go stale like the JSON descriptions can), and system_context() renders a file's architectural "system" (name, description, depends_on_systems/dependents_systems) aggregated from the systems object.

.claude/hooks/_sot_scope.py.description: Shared helper (not a hook itself): given a list of changed file paths, returns which RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims are potentially affected (claims whose evidence array references one of those files), and via affected_docs()/--docs, the union of those claims' docs arrays — other documentation files (never CLAUDE.md, which is slated to become a generated rendering of the SOT) that duplicate the same fact and might need a look. Used to scope the audit agent to a commit's actual diff instead of re-checking all ~215 claims every time. CLI mode reads paths from argv or stdin (one per line); pipeable from `git diff --name-only`.

.claude/hooks/_ty_check.py.description: Shared helper (not a hook itself) for the ty_diff_pre.py/ty_diff_post.py pair: runs `ty check` (Astral's Rust type checker) on a single .py file via subprocess, parses its concise-format output into diagnostic dicts, and provides a line/col-independent key for diffing diagnostics across an edit.

.claude/hooks/dependents_reminder.py.description: PostToolUse hook (Edit|Write): after editing a mapped file, reminds Claude which other files list it as a dependent, so knock-on effects (like an outdated test) aren't forgotten.

.claude/hooks/file_map_context.py.description: PreToolUse hook (Read|Edit|Write): before Claude touches a mapped file, surfaces its description plus depends_on/dependents from rayflow_file_map.json, the file's architectural "system" and which other systems depend on it (via _file_map.system_context), and for .py files also lists real function/class signatures extracted live via ast — always accurate regardless of whether the JSON description itself is stale.

.claude/hooks/prompt_file_hints.py.description: UserPromptSubmit hook: if the user's prompt names something matching a mapped file's basename (e.g. "validator", "NodeCard"), surfaces that file's description as additionalContext.

.claude/hooks/session_orientation.py.description: SessionStart hook: injects a one-line pointer to rayflow_file_map.json's existence (file count, root description) so a fresh session knows to consult it.

.claude/hooks/sot_guard.py.description: PreToolUse hook (Edit|Write): blocks direct edits to RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json unless RAYFLOW_SOT_UNLOCK=1 is set in the session's environment. Layer 1 of 2 in the SOT edit-blocking design (docs/issues_system.md §6.3) — fast, in-session feedback against casual edits; NOT the real guarantee, since a Bash command bypasses it entirely (only matches Edit/Write). Layer 2 is scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py.

.claude/hooks/stale_reminder.py.description: Stop hook: scans the session transcript for Edit/Write calls made since the last check; if any touched a mapped file, blocks once (decision:block, guarded by stop_hook_active) to remind the model to verify/update that file's description in rayflow_file_map.json before ending the turn, and to hint (same block) when the edit touches a system referenced in rayflow_scenarios.json's end-to-end narratives. On the very first Stop fire of a session it only records a baseline offset and reports nothing, instead of scanning the whole prior transcript.

.claude/hooks/staleness_guard.py.description: PostToolUse hook (Write|Bash): heuristically flags when rayflow_file_map.json may be out of date — a new file was written that isn't in the map, or a shell command looks like it deletes a file that is.

.claude/hooks/ty_diff_post.py.description: PostToolUse hook (Edit|Write, .py files): re-runs ty on the file just edited and diffs it against the pre-edit snapshot from ty_diff_pre.py, surfacing only newly introduced diagnostics as additionalContext. Diffing cancels out ty's known false positives on Ray's @ray.remote-decorated code (present in both snapshots), leaving genuine regressions. Advisory only, never blocks.

.claude/hooks/ty_diff_pre.py.description: PreToolUse hook (Edit|Write, .py files): snapshots ty's type diagnostics for a file before it's touched, storing them in a per-session temp file so the paired PostToolUse hook can report only diagnostics newly introduced by the edit. Advisory only; no-ops if ty isn't installed.

.claude/hooks/workflow_hints.py.description: UserPromptSubmit hook: matches the prompt against trigger phrases in rayflow_workflows.json and surfaces the matching workflow's checklist as additionalContext — the coarse-grained counterpart to prompt_file_hints.py's per-file matching.

.claude/settings.json.description: Project-level Claude Code settings (shared via git) — permissions and harness configuration for this repo. Sets `agent: rayflow-main`, so every new session in this repo loads that subagent definition as its main-thread system prompt/tool restrictions instead of Claude Code's default.

.claude/settings.local.json.description: Machine-local Claude Code settings override, not meant to be shared/committed conventions aside.

.claude/workflows/audit-sot-parallel.js.description: Claude Code Workflow tool script (deterministic multi-agent orchestration; .claude/workflows/ is a new file category alongside .claude/hooks/ and .claude/agents/, grouped into the same `hooks-infra` system since it serves the same repo-quality-tooling purpose via yet another mechanism). Parallelizes a full, unscoped audit of RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json: partitions its sections into 6 hardcoded groups (SECTION_GROUPS, balanced by claim count — hardcoded because the script itself has no filesystem access, so it can't compute the partition dynamically; each spawned agent reads RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json itself and filters by its group's section_ids), fans out one read-only rayflow-auditor instance per group (each returns structured proposed_issues per a JSON schema instead of writing, to avoid a race on the shared rayflow_issues.json), then runs a final consolidation-phase rayflow-issue-writer instance that re-reads the current rayflow_issues.json, dedupes proposed issues against already-open claim_ids and against each other, and performs the single atomic write. Complements scripts/run_sot_audit.py (which is diff-scoped to a commit via .claude/hooks/_sot_scope.py and runs one sequential auditor instance) for the case of a full manual audit across every claim.

.github/workflows/cla.yml.description: CLA Assistant workflow: asks external contributors to sign the CLA before a PR can merge; skips the check entirely for PRs opened by the maintainer.

.github/workflows/pypi.yaml.description: CI workflow that builds and publishes the package to PyPI on release.

.github/workflows/test.yml.description: CI workflow that runs the pytest suite on pushes/PRs.

.gitignore.description: Patterns for files/directories git should not track (build artifacts, virtualenvs, caches, etc.).

.mcp.json.description: Registers Rayflow's own MCP server (served at /mcp by a running `rayflow serve`) so Claude Code can use its tools directly.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.description: pre-commit framework config. Local-only hooks (no external repos): wires scripts/generate_claude_md.py at the pre-commit stage (id claude-md-generate — regenerates and re-stages CLAUDE.md), scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py at the pre-commit stage (id agents-generate — regenerates and re-stages the 21 .claude/agents/rayflow-<system>-specialist.md files), scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py at the commit-msg stage (Layer 2 of SOT edit-blocking, §6.3 of docs/issues_system.md), and scripts/run_sot_audit.py at the pre-commit stage (scoped auditor invocation, §6.2 — blocks the commit if the auditor finds a divergence). default_install_hook_types installs both pre-commit and commit-msg git hooks from a plain `pre-commit install`.

CLA.md.description: Contributor License Agreement: contributors keep copyright but grant the maintainer the right to relicense their contributions, including commercially.

CLAUDE.md.description: GENERATED FILE (scripts/generate_claude_md.py, pre-commit hook `claude-md-generate`, re-staged automatically on every commit if changed — never edit by hand, edits are silently overwritten at the next commit). Banner + rayflow_system_prompt.md verbatim + an index of RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json's sections (heading + claim count + pointer, not the claim text) + an index of rayflow_file_map.json's systems. See docs/claude_md_generation.md for the full design.

COMMERCIAL-LICENSE.md.description: Explains Rayflow's dual-license model (AGPL-3.0-or-later free for non-commercial use; a separate commercial license is required otherwise) and how to obtain one.

CONTRIBUTING.md.description: Contributor guide: dev setup, how the CLA bot signing flow works, PR guidelines.

LICENSE.description: Full text of the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0, Rayflow's open-source license.

MANIFEST.in.description: setuptools sdist manifest: excludes CLAUDE.md (a generated file, see docs/claude_md_generation.md) and its hand-maintained source rayflow_system_prompt.md, docs/, and the five generated LLM-orientation JSONs (rayflow_file_map.json, rayflow_workflows.json, rayflow_scenarios.json, RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json, rayflow_issues.json) from the published PyPI package — all internal tooling artifacts, not user docs.

RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json.description: Structured, section-organized claims about the rayflow codebase (schema_version 2), extracted from rayflow_system_prompt.md (its source_file) — each claim an object {id, text, evidence, docs}. id is '<section_id>#<slug>', stable and referenced by rayflow_issues.json's claim_ids. evidence is a best-effort array of repo-relative code paths (optionally '#symbol') supporting the claim. docs is a best-effort array of OTHER documentation files (README.md, a SKILL.md, guide.py, ...) that independently restate the same fact — CLAUDE.md and rayflow_system_prompt.md are both deliberately excluded from every claim's docs: CLAUDE.md because it's a generated mechanical re-render (scripts/generate_claude_md.py), rayflow_system_prompt.md because it's the source the claims were extracted FROM, not an independent restatement. Both evidence and docs empty mean 'not yet located', not 'false'. No known_issues array anymore (migrated to rayflow_issues.json) and no per-claim verification timestamps (would churn every audit run). Not auto-derived or hook-consumed by itself — read by scripts/generate_claude_md.py (index only, not full dump), read/written by the audit agent (.claude/agents/rayflow-auditor.md), and by hand.

README.md.description: Project overview, installation, quickstart, and license summary shown on GitHub and PyPI.

docs/analisis-conceptual.md.description: Long-form conceptual analysis (in Spanish) of Rayflow's execution model: the two superimposed planes (control/exec and data) and the core mental model behind the engine.

docs/claude_md_generation.md.description: Design doc (in Spanish, self-contained) for the CLAUDE.md generation mechanism: why CLAUDE.md became a generated file (banner + rayflow_system_prompt.md verbatim + SOT/file-map indices), why it stays git-tracked rather than gitignored (avoids a fresh-clone bootstrap problem entirely), why no edit-blocking was needed (unlike the SOT — CLAUDE.md just gets silently overwritten on the next commit, so blocking would be redundant), and the scripts/generate_claude_md.py mechanism wired as the `claude-md-generate` pre-commit hook.

docs/frontend.md.description: Quick-reference (in Spanish) inventory of the editor frontend's tech stack and component responsibilities.

docs/issues_system.md.description: Design doc (in Spanish, self-contained) for a documentation-audit system. RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json (v2, implemented: claims as {id, text, evidence, docs} — evidence links code, docs links OTHER documentation that duplicates the fact, CLAUDE.md deliberately excluded since it's slated to become a generated rendering of the SOT) and rayflow_issues.json (v1, implemented: open-issues-only queue, no resolved history in-file — git log is the log) linked by stable ids. The audit agent (.claude/agents/rayflow-auditor.md) is implemented and confirmed working end-to-end via `claude -p --agent rayflow-auditor` (§6.1). SOT direct-edit blocking is two layers: .claude/hooks/sot_guard.py (PreToolUse, session-scoped, bypassable) and scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py (pre-commit commit-msg stage, Sot-Change trailer, no bypass — §6.3). The pre-commit wiring for the auditor itself is also implemented: scripts/run_sot_audit.py (pre-commit stage, id sot-audit) scopes the staged diff via _sot_scope.py (exits free if nothing in scope), runs the auditor, and blocks the commit (exit 1) only if it wrote to rayflow_issues.json — fails open on infra problems (claude missing, timeout), never on the LLM simply finding nothing (§6.2). Explains why these stay separate files rather than merging with rayflow_file_map.json.

docs/propuesta-runcontext.md.description: Design proposal (in Spanish) that motivated reifying a flow execution as a `RunContext` object to isolate concurrent runs.

docs/rayflow.md.description: Historical v1 design document (in Spanish, explicitly marked obsolete) describing the original workflow-orchestration model, kept as an intent record.

pyproject.toml.description: Python package metadata: dependencies, build system, author/license info, pytest config, and package-data inclusion rules.

rayflow/__init__.py.description: Package entry point: re-exports the public Python API (load/unload/execute/serve_events/stop) and exposes rayflow.__version__ resolved from installed package metadata. run() was removed from both api.py and this export.

rayflow/__main__.py.description: Lets the package run as `python -m rayflow`, delegating to the CLI.

rayflow/api.py.description: High-level Python API: load/unload/execute/execute_async/reconnect_async/serve_events/stop — the functions used by the CLI, the REST routes, and the MCP tools to drive flows in and out of Ray. execute/execute_async take an optional `request` (RequestData envelope) forwarded to the entry node's ctx.request. load() pre-validates (build) before spawning any actor.

rayflow/build/__init__.py.description: Re-exports build() as the package's public symbol.

rayflow/build/validator.py.description: Validates a parsed FlowDef against the node catalog and produces an executable BuiltFlow: flattens CallFlow subflows into one namespace, checks type/wiring/cycle correctness, and either raises on first error (build()) or collects every error in one pass (validate_all()) for editor/MCP clients. _find_entry() picks the flow's sole @entry_node (meta.is_entry). _with_dynamic_pins() no longer injects flow.inputs as outputs — entries declare their own Input/Output; for entries without run() it mirrors each Input as a same-named Output (passthrough). _splice_subflow identifies the subflow's entry generically via catalog meta.is_entry (not by name) and re-exposes the entry's declared Inputs as the subflow boundary.

rayflow/claude_tools/__init__.py.description: Marks claude_tools as a package so setuptools' packages.find (include=['rayflow*']) picks it up automatically; no code, just the template assets `rayflow install claude-tools` copies.

rayflow/claude_tools/agents/rayflow-debu.description: Installable Claude Code subagent (end-user template): diagnoses why a user's flow fails/misbehaves. Deliberately restricted to read-only tools (Read/Grep/Glob + diagnostic-only MCP tools, no create_flow/update_flow/Write) so it can only report a diagnosis, never silently modify the flow it's investigating.

rayflow/claude_tools/mcp.json.description: Template copied to <cwd>/.mcp.json by `rayflow install claude-tools`, identical in shape to this repo's own .mcp.json — registers the local rayflow MCP server at http://localhost:8000/mcp/. Only written if the destination doesn't already have one.

rayflow/claude_tools/skills/rayflow-flow.description: Installable Claude Code skill (end-user template): the MCP tool loop for building/editing/testing a flow (get_guide -> list_nodes -> validate_flow iterate -> create_flow/update_flow -> test_flow/run_flow), CallFlow composition, event-reactive flows, and the HTTP request/response mechanism (OnStart's headers/query/body/method, ctx.set_response_status/set_response_header) for flows served via rayflow serve.

[DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone script (installable Claude Code skill asset, not imported by rayflow itself): runs a flow against a batch of test cases via HTTP against a running `rayflow serve`'s POST /editor/flows/{name}/test endpoint, printing PASS/FAIL/INFO per case and exiting 1 on any failure or connection error.

[DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone script (installable Claude Code skill asset): offline flow validator that runs the same logic as the MCP validate_flow tool (rayflow.schema.loader + rayflow.build.validator.validate_all) locally without a running server, printing {valid, errors, warnings} as JSON and exiting 1 if invalid.

rayflow/claude_tools/skills/rayflow-node.description: Installable Claude Code skill (end-user template, not for this repo's own dev process): how to create/edit a custom Rayflow node in a user's own project — decorator choice, pin contract, create_custom_node/update_custom_node_source MCP tools, hot reload.

[DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone script (installable Claude Code skill asset): locally validates a custom node source file's syntax and decorator/pin contract by executing it as a module and inspecting get_node_meta(), without touching the running server's live catalog; prints {ok, errors, nodes} as JSON.

[DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone script (installable Claude Code skill asset): scaffolds a Rayflow custom node class (decorator import, ExecInput/ExecOutput, Input/Output pins, run() boilerplate) from CLI-specified pin specs, optionally writing directly to custom_nodes/<Name>.py.

rayflow/cli/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the click CLI group as `main`.

rayflow/cli/main.py.description: Click-based CLI: `rayflow serve` initializes Ray and starts the REST/editor/MCP server; `rayflow install claude-tools` copies packaged Claude Code skills/agent templates (rayflow/claude_tools/) into the current working directory's .claude/ and drops a .mcp.json registering the local MCP server, for an end user's own project (not this repo's own dev tooling).

rayflow/editor/__init__.py.description: Empty package marker for the editor subpackage.

rayflow/editor/custom_nodes_routes.py.description: FastAPI router for CRUD on custom node .py files (create/read/update/delete) plus hot-reloading the node catalog from disk without restarting the server.

rayflow/editor/frontend/.gitignore.description: Standard Git ignore for Node.js projects, excludes build artifacts, node_modules, and editor temp files.

rayflow/editor/frontend/README.md.description: Project documentation describing the React + TypeScript + Vite template setup and ESLint configuration guidance.

rayflow/editor/frontend/components.json.description: shadcn/ui configuration file specifying component paths, aliases, and Tailwind CSS settings.

rayflow/editor/frontend/eslint.config.js.description: ESLint configuration enforcing TypeScript, React best practices, and React hooks linting rules.

rayflow/editor/frontend/index.html.description: HTML entry point that bootstraps the React app with root div and main.tsx script.

rayflow/editor/frontend/package-lock.jso.description: Generated npm lockfile pinning exact frontend dependency versions; not hand-edited.

rayflow/editor/frontend/package.json.description: Dependency manifest with React, xyflow, shadcn/ui, CodeMirror, Tailwind CSS, and build/lint scripts.

rayflow/editor/frontend/public/favicon.s.description: Browser tab icon in SVG format for the Rayflow editor application.

rayflow/editor/frontend/public/icons.svg.description: SVG sprite sheet containing reusable icon definitions for the UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/App.css.description: Global and template styles for the main app layout and hero section (legacy/boilerplate).

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/App.tsx.description: Main application component orchestrating the flow editor UI with header, sidebars, canvas, properties panel, and runs panel.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/hero..description: Promotional/hero image asset for the editor UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/react.description: React logo asset used in template/demo content.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/vite..description: Vite logo asset used in template/demo content.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/C.description: Full-screen code editor for editing Python custom node source with CodeMirror, syntax highlighting, and save functionality.
Collapsible sidebar panel for managing custom Python node files with create, edit, and delete operations.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/F.description: Visual flow graph editor using React Flow library, supporting node drag-drop, edge connections, live run visualization, and animation timing controls.

Modal dialog for editing the flow's declared output pins (type selection, duplicate name validation). No input pins here — a flow's inputs live on its entry node (OnStart, ChatTrigger, a custom @entry_node, ...), not on the flow itself; that's a Python-level concern, not something this JSON-shaped dialog edits.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/N.description: React Flow node component displaying node metadata, exec/data pins with type colors, runtime badges, and run status indicators.
Categorized sidebar panel for dragging built-in and custom nodes onto the canvas with search filtering.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/P.description: Right sidebar showing selected node details, editable literal inputs, connected pins, validation errors, and output specs.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/R.description: Bottom panel for flow execution with input parameter entry, run history, and live output/error display during execution.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/V.description: Left sidebar section for managing flow variables (name, type, default value) with inline editing.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/u.description: shadcn/ui Accordion primitive: expandable/collapsible sections using Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Badge primitive: small colored label/chip component with variant support.
shadcn/ui Button primitive: clickable button with variant and size options (default, outline, ghost, destructive).
shadcn/ui Dialog primitive: modal popup with header, content, and footer sections from Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Input primitive: text input field component with native HTML integration.
shadcn/ui ScrollArea primitive: custom scrollbar styling wrapper using Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Select primitive: dropdown menu component with trigger, content, and option items.
shadcn/ui Separator primitive: horizontal or vertical divider line.
shadcn/ui Tabs primitive: tabbed interface with list and content panels from Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Textarea primitive: multi-line text input field.
shadcn/ui Tooltip primitive: hover-activated floating label from Radix UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/hooks/useRes.description: Custom hook for draggable sidebar/panel resizing with localStorage persistence and min/max constraints.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/hooks/useRun.description: Custom hook managing flow execution via SSE streaming (requested explicitly via an `Accept: text/event-stream` header, the same content-negotiation mechanism any API caller uses), event batching for animation, and run reconnection on disconnect.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/index.css.description: Global styles defining dark theme color tokens, React Flow overrides, node card styling, and type color mapping.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/api.ts.description: API client layer defining TypeScript types and fetch wrappers for backend endpoints (flows, nodes, runs, custom nodes). runFlowUrl/reconnectRunUrl point at /flows/{name}/run(/...) in rayflow/server.py — the same run endpoint any external API caller would use, not an editor-only route.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/rayflowH.description: CodeMirror syntax highlighting and hover tooltips for Rayflow Python node decorators, pin declarations, and context methods.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/translat.description: Bidirectional converters between React Flow graph format and Rayflow FlowDef JSON, with exec pin logic and type color mapping.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/utils.ts.description: Utility functions exporting a className composition helper (cn) combining clsx and Tailwind merge.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/main.tsx.description: React app entry point that renders the root App component into the DOM.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/store/flowSt.description: Zustand store managing editor state including flow tabs, node/edge graph, runs, validation errors, and localStorage persistence.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.app.jso.description: TypeScript compiler config for the application source code with path aliases and strict type checking.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.json.description: Root TypeScript config referencing app and node build configs.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.node.js.description: TypeScript config for build tools and Vite configuration files.

rayflow/editor/frontend/vite.config.ts.description: Vite build configuration with React plugin, path aliases, API proxying, and build output to ../static/dist.

rayflow/editor/guide.py.description: Curated markdown guide to Rayflow's flow model, served by GET /editor/guide and the MCP get_guide tool as the semantic contract an LLM agent needs to build flows. Documents @entry_node (declares its own Input/Output pins, gets EntryContext with .request), auto-passthrough for entries without run(), ctx.set_response_status()/set_response_header() for flows served over HTTP.

rayflow/editor/routes.py.description: FastAPI router for the visual editor: node catalog, type info, flow CRUD, validation, load/test endpoints, guide, and event subscription. _node_spec() surfaces meta.is_entry. The _DYNAMIC_PINS table no longer includes OnStart/FlowInput/OnEvent — those nodes declare their pins statically now; only FlowOutput/Parallel/CallFlow remain as dynamic. run_flow_response accepts an optional `request` RequestData. The packaged-examples endpoints (GET /editor/examples, GET /editor/examples/{name}) were removed.

rayflow/editor/storage.py.description: Flow persistence: reads/writes flow JSON files under the workspace's flows/ directory and serializes a FlowDef back to a JSON-safe dict (no `inputs` field — entries declare their own Input pins).

rayflow/engine/__init__.py.description: Re-exports LoadedFlow (the engine's only public symbol after the registry moved to rayflow/registry.py).

rayflow/engine/executor.py.description: The core execution engine: the FlowEngine Ray actor (runs a BuiltFlow node-by-node, dispatching @ray_node/@engine_node/CallFlow nodes, resolving data pins, emitting SSE events) and LoadedFlow (lifecycle of a flow's actors in Ray). The entry node is no longer short-circuited: it runs the normal _fire_engine_node path, constructs an EntryContext (with .request) for the root entry only, and _resolve_inputs pulls the root entry's inputs from RunContext.flow_inputs (the HTTP body). Entries without run() get auto-passthrough (mirror Input→Output) and auto-fire exec_out if run() didn't fire. RunContext now carries flow_inputs + request; execute() takes a request arg.

rayflow/events/bus.py.description: The global EventBroker Ray actor: namespaced fire-and-forget pub/sub that lets one flow's EmitEvent trigger another flow's OnEvent/OnVariableChange.

rayflow/mcp/__init__.py.description: Re-exports create_mcp, the entry point for Rayflow's MCP layer.

rayflow/mcp/server.py.description: Builds the FastMCP server exposing the curated MCP tool set for LLM agents: discovery (get_guide/list_nodes/get_node/list_types/type_check), validation (validate_flow), flow CRUD (list/get/create/update/delete_flow, flow_catalog), custom-node CRUD with hot reload, events (serve_flow_events/stop_flow_events), and execution (run_flow/test_flow with optional trace, unload_flow). All reuse the editor REST logic via rayflow.api (which uses rayflow.registry internally) instead of reimplementing it. create_mcp() takes no parameters — served flows are read from the registry, not passed in.

rayflow/nodes/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the node-authoring API: decorators, pin descriptors, ExecContext, and NodeCatalog.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/__init__.py.description: Imports CallFlow so it's registered as part of the builtin module set.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/cast.py.description: Builtin explicit type-casting nodes: ToInt, ToFloat, ToStr, ToBool.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/chat_trigger_front.description: Static chat UI bundle for the built-in ChatTrigger entry node (declares frontend = "chat_trigger_frontend"); mounted at GET /flows/{name}/ui for any served flow whose entry is ChatTrigger. Self-contained HTML/CSS/JS that POSTs {message: ...} to /flows/{name}/run and renders the response — no build step, no separate transport.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/claude.py.description: [DRAFT sin revisar] Builtin @ray_node Claude node: invokes the Claude Code CLI (`claude -p --output-format json`) as a subprocess (not the Claude Agent SDK, to avoid pydantic/starlette/uvicorn deps), parsing its JSON envelope into result/structured_output/is_error/error/cost_usd/session_id outputs and firing success/failure exec pins; supports multi-turn conversations via resume_session_id.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/compare.py.description: Builtin pure comparison/boolean-logic nodes (no exec pins): GreaterThan, LessThan, Equal, NotEqual, Not, And, Or, etc.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/control.py.description: Builtin control-flow nodes: OnStart (entry, @entry_node, declares body/headers/query/method from the HTTP envelope as Inputs with empty defaults — auto-passthrough since no run()), ChatTrigger (entry with frontend bundle, declares message Input + message_out Output + run() that forwards), FlowOutput, Branch, Sequence, Parallel (fork/join), ForEach, While, Map. OnStart and ChatTrigger now use category="Trigger" (unified with OnEvent/OnVariableChange in events.py) instead of "Control". The EntryX/EntryXY/EntryAB/EntryN/EntryABC/EntryItems/EntryNumbersThreshold/EntryXBool convenience entries that used to live here were removed — re-homed as test-only fixtures in tests/entry_fixtures.py so they no longer ship in the real builtin catalog.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/events.py.description: Builtin event nodes: OnEvent (@entry_node triggered by the event bus; declares event_name config Input + payload Input, auto-passthrough), OnVariableChange (@entry_node triggered by a watched variable; declares variable/source config + value/old Inputs, auto-passthrough), and EmitEvent (@engine_node, publishes to the bus). OnEvent and OnVariableChange both use category="Trigger" (unified with OnStart/ChatTrigger in control.py).

rayflow/nodes/builtin/flow.py.description: The CallFlow builtin node: declares the pins for running a subflow, orchestrated directly by the engine rather than via its own run().

rayflow/nodes/builtin/variables.py.description: Builtin state nodes: Get (reads a GraphState variable) and Set (writes one).

rayflow/nodes/decorators.py.description: Defines the node-authoring contract: the Input/Output/ExecInput/ExecOutput pin descriptors, the @ray_node/@engine_node/@parallel_node/@entry_node decorators, NodeMeta/PinSpec metadata, the unified ExecContext used by every node's run(), and EntryContext (subclass with .request) used only by @entry_node classes. @entry_node marks a flow's entry point — sets NodeMeta.is_entry (read by _find_entry), rejects exec_in and missing ExecOutput, and is the only path to EntryContext/RequestData. NodeMeta.is_entry is set ONLY by @entry_node (no longer a class attribute authors set). The old exposes_flow_inputs flag is gone.

rayflow/nodes/loader.py.description: NodeCatalog: discovers and registers node classes from a directory or from the workspace's custom_nodes/ package, handling pickling concerns for Ray worker distribution.

rayflow/nodes/registry.py.description: Builds and caches the process-wide singleton NodeCatalog from the builtin node modules plus the workspace's custom nodes; reset_catalog() forces a rebuild for tests/hot-reload.

rayflow/registry.py.description: Process-wide registry of served flows — the single source of truth for 'which flows are currently served'. ServedFlow.interface() derives the public inputs from the entry node's declared Input pins (meta.inputs) instead of the removed flow.inputs field.

rayflow/schema/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the schema dataclasses (PinKind, PinDef, NodeDef, FlowDef, VariableDef) and load_flow.

rayflow/schema/loader.py.description: Parses a flow JSON (file, path, or dict) into a FlowDef (no `inputs` field — entries declare their own Input pins), and detects unknown/typo'd schema keys to surface as validation warnings. `inputs` is no longer a recognized top-level key.

rayflow/schema/models.py.description: The flow JSON schema's dataclasses: NodeDef, FlowDef (no longer has an `inputs` field — inputs live on the entry node's declared Input pins), VariableDef, PinDef/PinKind, including the internal fields flatten() uses to track CallFlow splicing.

rayflow/server.py.description: FastAPI app factory for `rayflow serve`: builds the REST API, mounts the editor router, the custom-nodes router, the MCP server at /mcp, and the static editor frontend. POST /flows/{name}/run builds a RequestData from the HTTP request and fuses it with the body into flow_inputs (so the entry's declared Input for `headers`/`query`/`body`/`method` is populated), forwarding both as the request envelope to the entry's ctx.request.

rayflow/state/actor.py.description: The GraphState Ray actor: a loaded flow's persistent named-variable memory across runs, plus variable-watch registration that publishes change events.

rayflow/state/queue.py.description: The RunQueue Ray actor: a per-flow FIFO event queue (one sub-queue per run_id) that the engine pushes to and FastAPI drains via blocking get() to stream SSE.

rayflow/types.py.description: The data-pin type system: closed registry of primitives (int/float/str/bool/list/dict/Any), generics (list[T], dict[str,V]), and strict compatibility checking with no implicit coercion.

rayflow/workspace.py.description: Working-directory conventions: locates/creates custom_nodes/ and flows/, builds the Ray runtime_env that distributes custom_nodes/ to workers, and resolves a flow name or path.

rayflow_agents_system.md.description: Design doc (in Spanish, self-contained) for programmatically generated per-system specialist subagents in .claude/agents/. IMPLEMENTED: scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py (pre-commit hook `agents-generate`) produces one rayflow-<system>-specialist.md per system in rayflow_file_map.json.systems (21, no curation), WITH embedded content (file descriptions, inter-system deps, relevant SOT claims text+evidence, open issues) — reversing the doc's original §2/§3 recommendation against embedding, since regenerating on every commit (same mechanism as CLAUDE.md, docs/claude_md_generation.md) removes the staleness risk that motivated it. Distinguishes this repo-dev tooling (.claude/agents/) from the shipped rayflow-debugger.md product template (rayflow/claude_tools/agents/). Frames synchronous multi-agent 'meetings' as an explicitly deferred future direction (§8). Open: per-specialist tool-access levels (§9).

rayflow_file_map.json.description: Generated flat map of every tracked repo file to a short English description plus depends_on/dependents edges (computed from real imports). Not shipped in the PyPI package. Consumed by the .claude/hooks/*.py scripts to give Claude Code per-file context automatically.

rayflow_issues.json.description: Open-issues-only queue produced by comparing RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims against the real repo state. Each issue links claim_ids (SOT), files (affected source), and docs (prose docs also out of date). No status/resolved_* fields and no suggested_fix (avoids biasing whoever picks it up) — 'resolving' an issue means deleting it from the array in the fixing commit; git history is the log, not this file. next_id is a monotonic counter, never reused even as the array shrinks. See docs/issues_system.md for the full design.

rayflow_scenarios.json.description: End-to-end scenario narratives describing how systems cooperate to accomplish something a user or LLM agent actually cares about (running a flow from the editor, an agent authoring via MCP, event-reactive flows, custom-node hot reload, CallFlow subflow splicing). Unlike depends_on_systems/dependents_systems, these can't be derived mechanically — they require an LLM to understand intent, and have no automated correctness check.

rayflow_system_prompt.md.description: Hand-maintained source of CLAUDE.md's prose. Deliberately high-level only: workflow conventions (test sandbox), dev commands, and the top-level architecture/design-principles mental map needed to route work to the right specialist — plus instructions for delegating per-system detail to the .claude/agents/rayflow-<system>-specialist subagents (spawn via Agent, then converse via SendMessage instead of re-reading files). Per-system detail (node system, engine internals, REST/MCP API tables, flow JSON schema, UI style rules) now lives only in those specialist agents, not here. CLAUDE.md itself is generated FROM this file — edit this one, not CLAUDE.md. See docs/claude_md_generation.md.

rayflow_workflows.json.description: Task-shaped checklists for recurring multi-file changes (adding a node type, changing the SSE event schema, adding an MCP tool, etc.), keyed by trigger phrases. Surfaced by workflow_hints.py when a user prompt matches, so the checklist arrives before the first edit rather than being discovered file-by-file.

scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py.description: commit-msg stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .pre-commit-config.yaml): if RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json is staged, requires a `Sot-Change: <reason>` trailer in the commit message or rejects the commit. Layer 2 of 2 in the SOT edit-blocking design (docs/issues_system.md §6.3) — the real enforcement, since it runs against git's staged state regardless of how the file was edited. No env-var bypass here on purpose (the trailer must be typed fresh each commit). Validated end-to-end in an isolated temp repo.

scripts/draft_file_map_entries.py.description: [DRAFT sin revisar] pre-commit stage hook (id `file-map-draft`): for every file in rayflow_file_map.json carrying a `_draft_candidate` stub, sends a single batched `claude -p --agent rayflow-router` call to confirm/correct its description and system, writes the resolved values back (prefixed '[DRAFT sin revisar] ') and re-stages the file; fails open (never blocks the commit) if claude is unavailable, times out, or returns unparsable output.

scripts/find_orphaned_claims.py.description: [DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone, non-hook diagnostic script: reports RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims whose evidence file paths are all missing from the repo (fully orphaned), skipping claims with empty evidence per the 'not located yet' convention; read-only, always exits 0, meant to be run by hand.

scripts/generate_claude_md.py.description: pre-commit stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .pre-commit-config.yaml, id `claude-md-generate`): regenerates CLAUDE.md from rayflow_system_prompt.md + an index of RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json sections + an index of rayflow_file_map.json systems. Deterministic, no LLM. If the regenerated content differs from what's on disk, overwrites CLAUDE.md and `git add`s it so the new version rides along in the commit being made. Never blocks (always exit 0). See docs/claude_md_generation.md.

scripts/generate_file_map.py.description: [DRAFT sin revisar] pre-commit stage hook (id `file-map-generate`, first in the generator chain): mechanically regenerates rayflow_file_map.json from `git ls-files` plus depends_on/dependents import edges parsed via tree-sitter for .py/.ts/.tsx files, stubbing brand-new files with a cheap no-LLM description/system candidate (`_draft_candidate`) for scripts/draft_file_map_entries.py to later confirm; fails open per-file and overall, never blocks the commit.

scripts/generate_macro_agents.py.description: [SIN VERIFICAR] Archivo nuevo (generate_macro_agents.py), sin docstring de modulo. Vecinos del mismo directorio -- check_sot_commit_message.py: commit-msg stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via . draft_file_map_entries.py: [DRAFT sin revisar] pre-commit stage hook (id `file-map-draft`): for every file in rayflow_file_map. find_orphaned_claims.py: [DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone, non-hook diagnostic script: reports RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH. generate_claude_md.py: pre-commit stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .

_draft_candidate.description: Archivo nuevo (generate_macro_agents.py), sin docstring de modulo. Vecinos del mismo directorio -- check_sot_commit_message.py: commit-msg stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via . draft_file_map_entries.py: [DRAFT sin revisar] pre-commit stage hook (id `file-map-draft`): for every file in rayflow_file_map. find_orphaned_claims.py: [DRAFT sin revisar] Standalone, non-hook diagnostic script: reports RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH. generate_claude_md.py: pre-commit stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .

scripts/generate_specialist_agents.py.description: pre-commit stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .pre-commit-config.yaml, id `agents-generate`): regenerates one .claude/agents/rayflow-<system>-specialist.md per system in rayflow_file_map.json.systems, with real embedded content — file descriptions, depends_on_systems/dependents_systems, the RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims whose evidence falls in that system's files (grouped by SOT section, text + evidence), and open rayflow_issues.json issues that mention the system. Deterministic, no LLM. Re-stages any file whose content changed so it rides along in the current commit. Never blocks. See rayflow_agents_system.md.

scripts/run_sot_audit.py.description: pre-commit stage hook (framework `pre-commit`, wired via .pre-commit-config.yaml): scopes RAYFLOW_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.json claims to this commit's staged diff via .claude/hooks/_sot_scope.py (exits immediately, no LLM call, if no claim's evidence overlaps the diff), then runs the rayflow-auditor subagent via `claude -p --agent rayflow-auditor "<prompt>"`. Blocks the commit (exit 1) only if the auditor writes new content to rayflow_issues.json — that's the one signal that means a real divergence was found, vs. the auditor simply failing to run (claude binary missing, timeout, non-zero exit with no file write), which fails open with a stderr warning instead of blocking on infra flakiness. Task #4 of docs/issues_system.md §6.2 — the counterpart to scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py: that one guards edits *to* the SOT, this one guards code changes that silently invalidate what the SOT already claims. Validated end-to-end: a real `claude -p --agent rayflow-auditor` run against a trivial, non-contradicting change exits 0 with no writes; the blocking branch (exit 1 on a rayflow_issues.json write) validated by simulating the subprocess call and file mutation.

signatures/cla.json.description: Generated file (written by the CLA Assistant GitHub Action) recording which external contributors have signed the CLA.

tests/__init__.py.description: Empty package marker for the test suite.

tests/entry_fixtures.py.description: Test-only @entry_node fixtures (EntryXY/EntryX/EntryAB/EntryN/EntryABC/EntryItems/EntryNumbersThreshold/EntryXBool) with register() to add them to the process-wide NodeCatalog. Re-homed here from rayflow/nodes/builtin/control.py so they never ship in the real catalog a `pip install rayflow` user sees; called from each test file's ray_init fixture right after reset_catalog(), since a catalog reset wipes anything outside rayflow.nodes.builtin.

tests/helpers.py.description: Test-only helper (run_once) replacing the removed rayflow.api.run(): loads, executes, and unconditionally unloads a flow — the always-fresh-reload behavior many tests rely on (reusing generic throwaway flow names like "parent"/"counter" across unrelated test functions), scoped to test code now that it's no longer part of the public API.

tests/test_build.py.description: Tests for the build/validation layer: BuildError cases for unknown node types, missing/duplicate entry nodes, missing exec inputs, type mismatches, and data cycles. Covers the generic @entry_node entry-point mechanism: any class decorated with @entry_node (not just the builtins) builds as the flow's entry, more than one entry node is a build error, and @entry_node is rejected when combined with @ray_node or when it declares exec_in.

tests/test_callflow.py.description: Tests for the CallFlow node: isolated vs. shared subflow state, meta['flow'] attribution, CallFlow inside a Parallel branch, and nested CallFlows.

tests/test_cli.py.description: Tests for `rayflow install claude-tools` via Click's CliRunner: fresh install writes skills/agent/.mcp.json, idempotency without --force, and --force overwriting skills/agent but never .mcp.json.

tests/test_custom_nodes.py.description: Tests for convention-based custom node discovery (custom_nodes/ + flows/ in the cwd) and the runtime_env it produces for Ray workers.

tests/test_editor.py.description: Tests for the /editor/* FastAPI routes: node catalog, types, validation (including multi-error collection and warnings), flow CRUD, custom nodes, concurrency isolation, events, and the self-verification /test endpoint. Also covers POST /flows/{name}/run (rayflow/server.py) from the editor-managed-flow side — requires an explicit POST /editor/flows/{name}/load first (no auto-load on demand: 'served' = 'explicitly loaded'), 404 if not served, SSE vs. JSON via the Accept header — since that endpoint is shared with served flows rather than living under /editor. The packaged-examples tests were removed along with that feature.

tests/test_events.py.description: Tests for the EventBroker: cross-flow EmitEvent/OnEvent pub/sub, OnVariableChange triggering on variable writes, and namespace isolation.

tests/test_executor.py.description: End-to-end execution tests with Ray: fan-out, Parallel fork/join (including nested), AND/OR exec_in join semantics, While/Map nodes, pure comparisons, per-run output isolation (the RunContext regression test), OnStart's request pins defaulting gracefully outside HTTP, ctx.set_response_status()/set_response_header() via both the engine_node local-closure and ray_node RPC paths, and custom @entry_node classes running as a flow's entry point (with and without declaring the HTTP envelope pins) exactly like OnStart/OnEvent do.

tests/test_mcp.py.description: Tests for the curated MCP tool layer (rayflow/mcp/server.py), exercised through FastMCP's in-memory client: discovery/validation/flow CRUD, the custom-node hot-reload lifecycle, the update_flow/delete_flow stale-graph regression, unload_flow, run_flow's trace=True, and serve/stop_flow_events.

tests/test_nodes.py.description: Tests for the node definition and discovery system: decorators, pin descriptors, NodeCatalog registration, and NodeMeta.is_entry being set only by the @entry_node decorator (false by default, rejecting exec_in, requiring at least one ExecOutput).

tests/test_schema.py.description: Tests for flow JSON schema deserialization (load_flow / FlowDef parsing).

tests/test_server.py.description: Tests for the REST API used by `rayflow serve` (rayflow/server.py): served-flow listing, running, duplicate-name validation, GraphState persistence across requests, OnStart's fixed headers/query/body/method outputs, ctx.set_response_status()/set_response_header() reaching the real HTTP response (including header isolation between separate requests), POST /flows/{name}/run returning 404 for an editor-managed flow that hasn't been explicitly served via POST /editor/flows/{name}/load (no on-demand auto-load — 'served' = 'explicitly loaded'), and the /flows/{name}/ui bundle (ChatTrigger end-to-end, 404 when the entry declares no frontend, UI disappearing after unload).

build.description: Validates a parsed FlowDef against the node catalog and produces an executable BuiltFlow: flattens CallFlow subflows into one namespace, checks type/wiring/cycle correctness, and either raises on first error or collects every error in one pass for editor/MCP clients.

ci.description: GitHub Actions workflows: CLA enforcement, test suite, and PyPI publishing.

cli.description: The `rayflow` command-line entry point (serve, and future subcommands) built on top of rayflow.api and rayflow.server.

docs.description: Long-form prose: README, CLAUDE.md (this repo's own agent-facing architecture guide), design-decision docs, and licensing/contribution documents (LICENSE, CLA.md, COMMERCIAL-LICENSE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md).

editor-api.description: The REST surface for the visual editor: flow CRUD, validation, catalog resolution, custom-node CRUD with hot reload, and the curated markdown guide served to LLM agents.

engine.description: The FlowEngine Ray actor and LoadedFlow lifecycle: executes a BuiltFlow node-by-node (sequential exec pins, parallel data pins), manages per-run scratch state (RunContext), and is the runtime core every other backend system ultimately drives.

events.description: The EventBroker pub/sub bus: fire-and-forget publish/subscribe connecting EmitEvent nodes, OnEvent/OnVariableChange entry points, and cross-flow event-triggered execution.

frontend-app.description: App shell: root component, entry point, global styles, static assets, and the HTML page the editor mounts into.

frontend-build.description: Frontend build/tooling config: Vite, TypeScript project references, ESLint, npm package manifest — governs how src/ compiles into rayflow/editor/static/dist/.

frontend-canvas.description: The React Flow graph canvas: node rendering (NodeCard), the FlowCanvas itself with execution animations, and the flowDef-JSON <-> React Flow nodes/edges translator.

frontend-panels.description: Sidebar/footer editor panels: node palette, variables panel, custom-nodes CodeMirror editor, properties panel, runs panel, and the flow settings dialog.

frontend-state.description: Client-side state and data access: the Zustand store (tabs, runs, catalog), the typed HTTP API client, and the SSE run-streaming hook with reconnect logic.

frontend-ui-kit.description: Design-system primitives adapted from shadcn/ui (Button, Dialog, Select, Tabs, etc.), rewritten to use inline styles instead of Tailwind per this repo's UI conventions.

hooks-infra.description: Repo-quality tooling infrastructure: Claude Code hooks that read rayflow_file_map.json to give an LLM agent per-file/per-system context, staleness reminders, live symbol/type-diagnostic info, and workflow checklists (plus settings.json wiring them in); the rayflow-auditor subagent and its _sot_scope.py helper; and the git/pre-commit-level SOT edit-blocking mechanism (scripts/check_sot_commit_message.py, .pre-commit-config.yaml) — a different mechanism (git hooks, not Claude Code hooks) serving the same repo-quality purpose.

mcp.description: The curated FastMCP tool layer exposing a subset of the editor API as MCP tools for LLM agents (get_guide, list_nodes, validate_flow, run_flow, etc.) plus the .mcp.json client registration for this repo.

nodes.description: The node system: @ray_node/@engine_node/@parallel_node decorators, pin descriptors (Input/Output/ExecInput/ExecOutput), NodeCatalog discovery/loading, and the built-in node library (math, control flow, casting, comparisons, variables, events).

packaging.description: Controls what actually ships: pyproject.toml package metadata/dependencies, MANIFEST.in inclusion/exclusion rules, and the built frontend bundle (rayflow/editor/static/dist/) that server.py serves.

schema.description: Flow JSON schema (FlowDef/NodeDef plain stdlib @dataclass models, not Pydantic — no pydantic dependency exists in this project) and the canonical data-pin type system (int/str/list[T]/dict[str,V]/Any) with compatibility rules. Foundational — almost every other backend system imports from here.

server.description: Top-level app wiring and the public Python API: FastAPI app assembly (rayflow/server.py), and rayflow.api's load()/execute()/execute_async()/serve_events()/stop() functions that everything else (CLI, tests, external callers) goes through. run() was removed — no more load+run+unload one-shot in the public API (tests/helpers.py's run_once is a test-only replacement, not public).

state.description: Two detached Ray actors that persist across executions of a loaded flow: GraphState (variables + watch_variable for change-triggered flows) and RunQueue (per-run SSE event sub-queues consumed by FastAPI).

tests.description: The pytest suite covering build/validation, the engine/executor, the editor REST API, custom nodes, events, CallFlow subflows, MCP tools, node behavior, and schema parsing.
